prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark, nearly black frame, with no clearly visible objects or people.

By around 6 seconds, the scene has changed to a studio-like setting with a plain light gray background. A man is shown from about the waist up, centered in frame. He is dressed in a black suit jacket, white shirt, bow tie, bowler hat, and white gloves, with a small dark moustache. His styling resembles an old silent-film comic character. His hands are raised near his chest, suggesting a performed gesture or mime-like movement.

By the final sampled frame, the setting is a wide, minimal studio space with a pale floor and background. The same man stands near the center-right, holding or standing beside a long vertical strip of paper or printed material extending upward. To his left is a small round pedestal table with a personal computer on it; the monitor is on and shows a blue screen. On the wall/background to the right of the man is large text reading:

“IBM  
Personal  
Computer  
Software”

The final composition presents the performer, the computer, and the IBM Personal Computer Software text together in a clean product-display arrangement. No audio, dialogue, slogan, or music can be determined from the frames.
